channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: U.S. launches strikes on Iran in response to attacks on American warships, military says
item.description: The “self-defense strikes” followed attacks on three American naval vessels, though none of the warships were hit, U.S. Central Command said in a statement.

item.title: Correction: Trump-appointed panel calls for overhauling how FEMA operates. An earlier alert incorrectly said the report included plans to cut staffing in half.
item.description:
In its final report, the FEMA Review Council also recommended shifting leadership of emergency response and recovery to the states.
An earlier version of this alert incorrectly said the report included plans to cut staffing in half. This alert has been corrected.

item.title: Trade court delivers new blow to President Donald Trump in his effort to impose widespread tariffs
item.description: The Court of International Trade ruled against the import tax the president imposed after losing on tariffs at the Supreme Court.

item.title: Sen. John Fetterman: I haven’t changed. Here’s what has.
item.description: John Fetterman: I haven’t changed. Here’s what has.

item.title: Authorities scramble to limit hantavirus outbreak, trace contacts around globe
item.description: U.S. officials in at least five states — Arizona, California, Georgia, Texas and Virginia — are monitoring symptoms of seven returning passengers.

item.title: Trump-appointed panel calls for slashing FEMA in half
item.description: In its final report, the FEMA Review Council also recommended shifting leadership of emergency response and recovery to the states.

item.title: The greatest danger aboard the hantavirus cruise ship is global
item.description: The hantavirus outbreak is a warning the world cannot ignore

item.title: Tennessee House approves new congressional map meant to give GOP an advantage after Supreme Court weakened the Voting Rights Act
item.description: The state’s action comes as Republican counterparts in Alabama, Louisiana and South Carolina push to carve up their majority-minority districts ahead of the midterm elections in November.

item.title: Suspect in Colorado firebombing attack pleads guilty
item.description: Mohamed Sabry Soliman, 46, faces life in prison for hurling molotov cocktails at pro-Israel demonstrators in June, killing one person and injuring others.

item.title: U.S. intelligence says Iran can outlast Trump’s Hormuz blockade for months
item.description: A confidential CIA assessment delivered to the White House finds Iran can withstand the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz for months and retains a substantial missile and drone arsenal.

item.title: What really killed Mozart? You don’t want to know.
item.description: “Amadeus” returns to TV and shows why genius always demands an antagonist.

item.title: A media giant in today’s On the Record
item.description: A media giant in today’s On the Record

item.title: She was once a ‘foot soldier’ in Trump’s online army. Now she’s torching it.
item.description: Once a prominent pro-Trump provocateur, Ashley St. Clair now claims to expose how top MAGA creators coordinate messaging and turn political outrage into profit.

item.title: Iran talks. Epstein note. Senate control. Hantavirus outbreak. Ted Turner. El Niño. Brain supplements. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: A pro-Kremlin lawyer’s turn against Putin reveals a rift in Russian power circles
item.description: Ilya Remeslo was put in a psychiatric hospital after criticizing Vladimir Putin. Now free, he said that he will not stop his crusade against the Russian president. His turnabout and continuing open defiance signify a broader divide in the upper reaches of Kremlin power, according to a Russian official, a prominent opposition figure and analysts.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein’s purported suicide note is released by court order
item.description: “It is a treat to be able to choose one’s time to say goodbye," reads part of the note, which was found by a former cellmate and had been kept under court seal for years. No court or investigative agency has vouched for its authenticity.

item.title: Judge denies local officials' request to return 2020 ballots seized by FBI from Fulton County, Georgia
item.description: The ruling clears the way for the Justice Department to move forward with its inquiry into Donald Trump's election loss to Joe Biden.

item.title: Justice Department launches civil rights probe of Virginia prosecutor over handling of immigrant defendants
item.description: Fairfax County Commonwealth’s Attorney Steve Descano, the Democratic prosecutor of Virginia’s largest county, has long faced attacks from Republicans who say he's too lenient with criminal defendants who are in the country illegally.

item.title: He wrote about their daughter’s sudden death. Then his wife did the same.
item.description: For journalists Danielle Crittenden and David Frum, processing their grief meant telling Miranda’s story, in an essay in the Atlantic and now in a memoir, “Dispatches From Grief.”

item.title: Photos show man firing at Secret Service near JD Vance’s motorcade
item.description: Documents filed in U.S. District Court in D.C. on Wednesday include moment-by-moment photographs of what happened during the shooting this week near the Washington Monument. A Texas man was charged.

item.title: Why is Trump backing off San Francisco? These results.
item.description: Why is Trump backing off San Francisco? These results.

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut suburbs, threatening already shaky ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli forces launched an airstrike against a Hezbollah commander in Beirut’s southern suburbs, the first attack near the Lebanese capital since the start of a U.S.-brokered ceasefire last month. The sides had continued to exchange fire in southern Lebanon, but Israel had refrained from striking Beirut, and Hezbollah had refrained from attacking Israeli territory.

item.title: The odds keep rising for the strongest El Niño in a century
item.description: For a third consecutive month, multiple models have predicted that a potentially record-breaking El Niño could drive global temperatures to new highs and shift patterns of droughts, floods, heat, humidity and sea ice across the planet. The chances of a powerful El Niño were boosted by a rare triplet cyclone pattern in the Pacific last month.

item.title: ICE plans to scrap shortened training for new immigration officers, returning to longer instruction period
item.description: The reduced training period drew criticism from Democrats and former federal law enforcement officials, who questioned whether new recruits were being rushed onto the job without adequate preparation at a time when the Trump administration is seeking to detain and deport record numbers of undocumented immigrants.

item.title: FBI raids offices and business of Virginia state Sen. L. Louise Lucas
item.description: The actions were part of an ongoing corruption investigation, according to two federal law enforcement officials familiar with the matter. The exact nature and targets of the probe remain unclear, though the officials said it involved allegations of bribery.

item.title: My daughter basically abandons me on hikes. Carolyn Hax readers give advice.
item.description: My daughter basically abandons me on hikes. Carolyn Hax readers give advice.

item.title: Americans broadly reject recent religion-related messages from Trump and Hegseth, Post poll finds
item.description: A majority of Americans have negative views of the president’s social media post appearing to depict himself as Jesus and the defense secretary’s prayer at the Pentagon for “overwhelming violence of action against those who deserve no mercy,” according to a Washington Post-ABC News-Ipsos poll. The survey finds positive ratings for Pope Leo XIV, who has criticized U.S. actions on immigration and in Iran.

item.title: How guaranteed are restaurant reservations? Ask our food critic your dining questions.
item.description: How guaranteed are restaurant reservations? Ask our food critic your dining questions.

item.title: What took down Spirit Airlines? Economists have a term for it.
item.description: The company’s natural death offers a case study in creative destruction.

item.title: Ted Turner, cable TV visionary who created CNN, dies at 87
item.description: The tycoon’s sprawling legacy encompassed conservation, philanthropy and professional sports, and his bravado earned him the nickname “Mouth of the South.” His “superstation” TBS was a cornerstone of cable TV’s early success, and his 24-hour news channel CNN revolutionized TV journalism.

item.title: After defying Trump, a Republican lawmaker hangs on by a thread
item.description: After resisting the president’s redistricting push in Indiana, Spencer Deery and six of his fellow Republican state senators faced primary challenges from Trump-endorsed opponents. The races have tested how much dissent GOP voters could tolerate.

item.title: A long battle in today’s On the Record
item.description: A long battle in today’s On the Record

item.title: Iran has hit far more U.S. military assets than reported, satellite images show
item.description: Imagery published by Iranian state-affiliated media and verified by The Post shows damage to at least 228 structures or pieces of equipment at U.S. military sites.

item.title: Iran war. Pope Leo. Indiana primaries. Vaccine studies. New York Times. Screen time. Depression therapy. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Former ICE deputy director Madison Sheahan loses GOP congressional primary in Ohio
item.description: Sheahan left her job as deputy director at U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement in January, hoping that her service in support of President Donald Trump’s top domestic policy issue would propel her to the nomination in Ohio’s 9th District. She was in third among five GOP candidates, with more than half the vote counted.

item.title: In a first, FDA authorizes fruit-flavored vapes for adults
item.description: The approval marks a shift after the FDA for years worked aggressively to regulate flavored vapes and came after President Donald Trump reportedly pressed the FDA commissioner to approve the product.

item.title: They thought their disabled daughter was safe. A pregnancy revealed her rape.
item.description: She’s blind and can barely speak, and her family is suing her Maryland group home, caregivers and state agencies. “The whole system failed Kamryn,” her mother says.

item.title: Activist who gave out fliers with Stephen Miller’s address won’t face charges
item.description: A prosecutor in Virginia cited “insufficient evidence” in the case involving the White House aide and said proceeding could have a “chilling effect on others wishing to engage in peaceful political protest.”

item.title: Chicago schools’ May Day field trip was wildly inappropriate
item.description: Sending students to political rallies undercuts parents' trust in teachers.

item.title: Federal agency sues New York Times, accusing it of bias against White male employee
item.description: The Equal Employment Opportunity Commission alleged that the newspaper violated civil rights law by denying a promotion to a White man and blamed its adherence to DEI policies.

item.title: Snubs and surprises from the Tony Award nominations
item.description: “Schmigadoon!,” “The Lost Boys” and “Ragtime” racked up the nods, while Lea Michele and Adrien Brody got snubbed.

item.title: Senate Republicans offer $1 billion for White House security, sparking dispute over Trump’s ballroom
item.description: Lawmakers maintain their proposal would authorize security construction, but not President Donald Trump’s ballroom. The White House disagrees.

item.title: Carolyn Hax: Empty nester bereft without ‘measured chaos’ of child-rearing
item.description: Introverted husband, friends and hobbies can’t give the extroverted letter writer enough to fill the next chapter of life.

item.title: Sen. Dick Durbin: Yes, Trump is deporting ‘dreamers.’ Now here’s the quiet part.
item.description: Processing holds. Delayed renewals. Much of the attack on DACA is hiding in plain sight.

item.title: The 9 best-dressed celebrities at the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: Attendees paid homage to artworks, to artists, to artistic concepts. Here are our favorite looks of the night.

item.title: A prominent lawyer in today’s On the Record
item.description: A prominent lawyer in today’s On the Record

item.title: Hegseth says U.S. mission to protect commercial ships in Strait of Hormuz is temporary; ceasefire with Iran remains in place
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the responsibility of protecting ships would be soon handed to other nations, emphasizing the fragile ceasefire remained in place despite attacks on U.S. ships on Monday.

item.title: Iran war. Midterms prep. White House lockdown. Abortion pills. Lurking microplastics. Cellphone bans. Met Gala. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Celebrities hit the Met Gala's red carpet as protesters gathered to highlight wealth inequality. Follow our coverage.
item.description: This year’s annual event will christen the new Condé M. Nast Galleries at the Met. Protesters were outside the entrance to the gala, and New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani, a democratic socialist, has said he’s skipping it to maintain his focus on affordability.

item.title: A GOP lawmaker supported an immigration crackdown. Her husband paid a price.
item.description: The situation cost Idaho state Sen. Glenneda Zuiderveld and her husband most of their income and highlights an escalating split in the Republican Party.
